hero_description: Master essential cybersecurity principles and digital safety practices in this foundation-level certification course. Designed for professionals and individuals seeking to protect themselves and their organizations from cyber threats in the Indian digital ecosystem. Gain practical knowledge of threat identification, data protection, and secure online practices applicable across all sectors.
overview_description: This comprehensive 8-week program covers fundamental cybersecurity concepts, threat landscapes, password security, malware awareness, phishing prevention, data protection, secure communication, and incident response basics. Participants will learn to identify vulnerabilities, implement security best practices, and develop a security-conscious mindset essential for working in any digital environment in India's rapidly evolving tech sector.

modules.title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals & Threat Landscape
topics: Introduction to Cybersecurity — definition, importance, and evolution in Indian context
CIA Triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability) — core security principles
Types of Cyber Threats — malware, ransomware, spyware, trojans, worms explained
Threat Actors & Motivations — hackers, cybercriminals, nation-states, insiders in India
Common Attack Vectors — email, web, social engineering, physical threats
Cybersecurity Statistics in India — current threat landscape and industry data
Regulatory Framework — GDPR, India's Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, IT Act 2000
Security Roles & Responsibilities — understanding who protects data in organizations
Impact of Cyber Attacks — financial, reputational, operational consequences for Indian businesses
Cybersecurity Awareness Importance — why every employee must understand security

modules.title: Authentication, Access Control & Password Security
topics: Authentication Basics — what it is and why it matters
Password Creation Best Practices — length, complexity, character types, avoiding common mistakes
Password Management Tools — password managers, secure storage, synchronization
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) — 2FA, 3FA, biometric authentication, security keys
Common Password Mistakes — reusing passwords, sharing credentials, writing down passwords
Biometric Authentication — fingerprint, facial recognition, iris scanning in Indian banking
Single Sign-On (SSO) — benefits and risks in enterprise environments
Access Control Models — role-based, attribute-based, principle of least privilege
Account Lockout Policies — protecting against brute force attacks
Password Recovery & Reset — secure procedures in corporate environments
Mobile Authentication — PIN, pattern, biometric on smartphones used in India

modules.title: Malware, Ransomware & Endpoint Security
topics: Malware Definition & Types — viruses, worms, trojans, rootkits, adware explained
Ransomware Attacks — how they work, payment demands, recovery strategies
Spyware & Adware — tracking, unwanted advertising, data harvesting threats
Cryptolockers & Encryption-Based Attacks — recent Indian incidents and prevention
Antivirus & Anti-Malware Software — selection, installation, regular updates
Endpoint Protection Platform (EPP) — modern defense layers for devices
Behavioral Analysis & Heuristic Detection — identifying unknown threats
File Scanning & Quarantine Procedures — safe handling of suspicious files
USB & Removable Media Risks — preventing malware spread through external devices
Mobile Malware — threats on Android and iOS devices commonly used in India
Safe Browsing Practices — avoiding malware-infected websites, drive-by downloads
Recovery from Malware Infection — steps to clean infected systems

modules.title: Phishing, Social Engineering & Email Security
topics: Phishing Attacks — definition, methods, and real-world examples from Indian banks
Email Phishing Red Flags — suspicious sender addresses, urgent language, links analysis
Spear Phishing — targeted attacks against specific individuals or organizations
Whaling Attacks — targeting executives and high-value employees in Indian companies
Social Engineering Tactics — pretexting, baiting, tailgating, quid pro quo
Vishing & Smishing — voice phishing and SMS-based social engineering in India
Phishing Simulation & Training — how organizations test employee awareness
Email Security Best Practices — verification protocols, checking sender addresses
Attachment Safety — scanning, safe file types, executable file risks
URL Shorteners & Link Verification — identifying hidden malicious URLs
Reporting Phishing Emails — internal procedures and national reporting mechanisms
Business Email Compromise (BEC) — invoice fraud, CEO fraud in Indian organizations

modules.title: Data Protection, Privacy & Secure Communication
topics: Data Classification — public, internal, confidential, restricted data levels
Personal Data & PII — identification, handling, protection requirements in India
Data Encryption — symmetric, asymmetric, end-to-end encryption basics
HTTPS & SSL/TLS — secure web connections, padlock indicators, certificate validation
VPN Usage — virtual private networks, benefits, risks, choosing legitimate VPN providers
Public WiFi Security Risks — unsecured networks, man-in-the-middle attacks
Secure Messaging — encrypted chat applications, Signal, WhatsApp encryption
File Encryption — encrypting sensitive documents, bitlocker, device encryption
Backup & Disaster Recovery — regular backups, off-site storage, recovery procedures
Data Retention Policies — how long to keep data, secure deletion methods
Privacy Settings — social media, cloud services, mobile device privacy configuration
GDPR & India's DPDP Act — compliance requirements for handling personal data

modules.title: Incident Response, Security Updates & Continuous Protection
topics: Security Incident Definition — what constitutes a security incident, severity levels
Incident Response Procedures — detection, containment, eradication, recovery
Reporting Security Incidents — internal escalation paths, documentation requirements
Password Compromise Response — immediate password change, account monitoring
Device Compromise Detection — unusual behavior, performance issues, malware signs
Data Breach Notification — legal requirements, Indian data protection laws
Forensics Basics — preserving evidence, timeline reconstruction, investigation support
Software Updates & Patching — importance, scheduling, zero-day vulnerabilities
Operating System Security — Windows, macOS, Linux security features and hardening
Browser Security — extensions, plugins, cache clearing, privacy mode usage
IoT Device Security — smart home devices, printers, cameras in Indian households
Continuous Security Awareness — staying updated, new threat trends, professional development

assessment.theory_format: 25 Questions x 2 Marks (Multiple Choice, True/False, Short Answer)
assessment.practical_format: Hands-on security scenarios, threat identification exercises, and security implementation tasks

practical_items.title: Threat Identification & Analysis
practical_items.description: Candidate must identify security threats in 5 simulated scenarios (phishing emails, malicious websites, suspicious files, social engineering attempts, network threats) and document findings with recommended actions

practical_items.title: Security Configuration & Implementation
practical_items.description: Candidate must configure password policies, enable MFA on a sample account, set up encryption on a device, and document security settings with screenshots and explanations

practical_items.title: Incident Response Simulation
practical_items.description: Candidate must respond to a simulated security incident by identifying the threat, containing it, documenting the timeline, and creating an incident report following organizational procedures

practical_items.title: Security Awareness Presentation
practical_items.description: Candidate must prepare and deliver a 5-minute presentation on one cybersecurity topic (phishing awareness, password security, malware prevention, or data protection) with visual aids

eligibility: Minimum education: Class 8th pass or equivalent
Basic computer literacy and internet access required
Already working in IT/tech sector? Apply for Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) exam
Valid government-issued ID proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport) required at registration
Applicable for all candidates across all sectors and geographies in India
learning_outcomes: Understand fundamental cybersecurity concepts, threat landscape, and risk assessment in Indian organizations
Create and manage strong passwords, implement multi-factor authentication, and control access effectively
Identify malware, ransomware, and endpoint threats; implement protective measures on devices
Recognize phishing, social engineering, and email-based attacks; respond appropriately to threats
Classify data, implement encryption, protect personal information, and ensure secure communication
Respond to security incidents, maintain system updates, and sustain continuous security awareness practices
course_features: Comprehensive coverage of cybersecurity fundamentals aligned with international standards
Real-world Indian case studies and threat scenarios from major incidents
Interactive practical exercises simulating actual security threats and responses
Hands-on labs for password management, encryption, and security configuration
Career guidance and job placement support for certified professionals
Access to updated learning materials reflecting latest threat trends in India
Flexible online and offline delivery options across India
industry_relevance: Applicable across all sectors — banking, finance, healthcare, government, IT, retail, manufacturing
Meets compliance requirements for organizations handling sensitive data in India
Supports mandatory cybersecurity awareness training in Indian enterprises
Aligns with National Cybersecurity Strategy 2020 and Digital India initiatives
Recognized by NASSCOM, DSCI, and other IT industry bodies in India
Essential foundation for advancing to advanced cybersecurity certifications

course_duration_breakdown.week_5: Phishing & Social Engineering (Part 2) + Data Protection (Part 1) — 13 hours
course_duration_breakdown.week_8: Incident Response & Updates (Part 2) + Final Revision & Assessment — 15 hours

teaching_methodology: Interactive classroom lectures with real-world examples from Indian organizations
Video demonstrations of threat identification and security practices
Hands-on practical labs using industry-standard tools and platforms
Phishing simulation exercises and security awareness games
Case study analysis of major cyber incidents affecting Indian businesses
Guest lectures from cybersecurity professionals working in India
Group discussions and peer learning sessions
Quizzes and formative assessments throughout the course
Final practical examination and written assessment
tools_and_platforms_covered: MFA applications (Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, Authy)
Email security tools (Gmail security features, Outlook)
Browser security extensions (uBlock Origin, HTTPS Everywhere)
Security awareness training platforms (Proofpoint, KnowBe4 simulation)
post_certification_support: Digital certificate and badge for LinkedIn profile
Lifetime access to updated course materials and resources
Monthly cybersecurity awareness newsletter with latest threats
Access to alumni network and professional community
Guidance for pursuing advanced certifications (CEH, CISSP, Security+)
Job portal access with cybersecurity-specific opportunities
Continued professional development through webinars and workshops
Discount on advanced courses and specializations
